chunks.title: Cancelling Your Subscription
chunks.text:
You can cancel a paid Nimbus subscription at any time. A workspace owner or billing admin can do it from Settings → Billing → Cancel subscription.

What happens when you cancel:

chunks.title: Cancelling Your Subscription
chunks.text:
- You keep full access to your paid features until the **end of the current billing period**. Cancelling stops the next renewal — it doesn't cut you off immediately.
- When the period ends, the workspace automatically moves to the **Starter (Free)** plan. Nothing is deleted; anything above Starter's limits becomes read-only until you're back under them or upgrade again.
- We don't pro-rate or refund the remainder of a period you've already paid for. (See the refund policy for the first-subscription money-back guarantee.)

On annual plans, cancelling stops the renewal at the end of your paid term; you keep access for the rest of that year.

chunks.title: Cancelling Your Subscription
chunks.text: Your data is retained after cancellation so you can reactivate later. If you want it removed, request deletion from Settings → Data & Privacy; we recommend exporting first. We permanently delete cancelled-workspace data 12 months after the plan ends unless you ask sooner.

chunks.title: Exporting Your Data
chunks.text:
You can export your workspace data at any time, on any plan, including Starter (Free). Workspace admins can run an export from Settings → Data & Privacy → Export data.

Export formats:

- **CSV** — tasks and projects as spreadsheets, ideal for analysis in Excel or Google Sheets.
- **JSON** — a complete structured export of the workspace (projects, tasks, comments, and metadata), ideal for migrating or backing up.

File attachments are included as a separate ZIP archive.

For small workspaces the export downloads immediately. For larger workspaces we generate it in the background and email a secure download link, usually within a few hours. That link is valid for 7 days.

chunks.title: Exporting Your Data
chunks.text: Exports reflect the data at the moment you start them. If you need a personal data report for a GDPR or CCPA request, the same export covers it; contact support if you need anything additional or a signed data-processing agreement.

chunks.title: Integrations
chunks.text:
Nimbus connects to the tools your team already uses. Set them up from Settings → Integrations, then authorize the connection.

Available integrations:

chunks.title: Integrations
chunks.text:
- **Slack** — get task and project notifications in channels, and create Nimbus tasks from messages.
- **Google Drive** — attach and preview Drive files on tasks.
- **GitHub** and **GitLab** — link commits and pull requests to tasks and auto-update status.
- **Figma** — embed live design previews on tasks.
- **Zoom** — schedule and join project calls from Nimbus.
- **Jira** — two-way sync of issues for teams migrating or running both tools.
- **Zapier** — connect Nimbus to 6,000+ other apps with no code.
- **REST API & webhooks** — build custom integrations and react to events in real time.

chunks.title: Integrations
chunks.text:
**Plan availability:** Slack and Google Drive are available on every plan, including Starter (Free). GitHub, GitLab, Figma, Zoom, Jira, Zapier, and the REST API & webhooks require Pro or higher. SSO/SAML is a Business feature.

Each integration can be connected once per workspace and managed by workspace admins.

chunks.title: Plan Limits & Seats
chunks.text:
A **seat** is a billable workspace member. On paid plans you're charged per seat, per billing cycle. Manage seats from Settings → Members.

**Member and project limits by plan:**

- **Starter (Free):** up to 5 members and 3 active projects.
- **Pro:** up to 50 members and unlimited projects.
- **Business:** unlimited members and unlimited projects.
- **Enterprise:** unlimited, with volume pricing.

**Guests** (view-only collaborators outside your team) don't count as seats. Pro includes up to 10 guests; Business and Enterprise include unlimited guests.

chunks.title: Plan Limits & Seats
chunks.text:
**Adding seats:** when you invite a member beyond your current seat count, the new seat is added immediately and prorated for the rest of the current billing cycle.

**Removing seats:** when you remove a member, their seat is freed at your next renewal, so you keep the capacity you've already paid for until then. Your renewal is then billed at the lower seat count.

If you hit a plan limit — for example a 6th member on Starter — Nimbus will prompt you to upgrade rather than blocking your work.

chunks.title: Pricing & Plans
chunks.text:
Nimbus has four plans: Starter, Pro, Business, and Enterprise.

**Starter** is free forever. It includes one workspace, up to 3 active projects, up to 5 members, 100 MB of file storage, and community support. It's meant for individuals and small teams trying Nimbus out.

**Pro** is $12 per user / month billed monthly, or $10 per user / month billed annually. It adds unlimited projects, up to 50 members, 10 GB of storage per user, all standard integrations, and priority email support.

chunks.title: Pricing & Plans
chunks.text:
**Business** is $22 per user / month billed monthly, or $18 per user / month billed annually. It includes everything in Pro plus advanced permissions, SSO/SAML, audit logs, 100 GB of storage per user, and priority support with a 1-business-day response SLA.

**Enterprise** is custom-priced for large organizations. It adds a dedicated customer success manager, custom contracts and invoicing, unlimited storage, and a 99.9% uptime SLA. Contact sales for a quote.

All paid plans are billed per active seat. You can switch between monthly and annual billing at any time from Settings → Billing; switching to annual applies the discounted rate at your next renewal.

chunks.title: Refund Policy
chunks.text:
Nimbus offers a money-back guarantee on your first paid subscription. If you're on a monthly plan, you can request a full refund within 14 days of your initial charge. On an annual plan, the window is 30 days from the initial charge.

Refunds are issued to the original payment method. Once approved, they typically take 5–10 business days to appear, depending on your bank or card issuer.

chunks.title: Refund Policy
chunks.text:
Renewal charges (the second and later billing cycles) are generally non-refundable, because you can cancel at any time before a renewal to avoid being charged. That said, if you were charged for a renewal you didn't intend — for example, you meant to cancel but missed the date — contact support and we'll review it case by case.

We don't pro-rate refunds for partial billing periods. If you cancel mid-cycle, you keep access until the end of the period you already paid for.

To request a refund, reach out to support with the email on your account and the approximate charge date.

chunks.title: Reset Your Password
chunks.text:
To reset your Nimbus password:

1. Go to the login page at app.nimbus.com/login.
2. Click **Forgot password?** below the sign-in form.
3. Enter the email address on your account and submit.
4. Open the reset email and click the link. The link is valid for 60 minutes.
5. Choose a new password (at least 10 characters) and sign in.

If your team uses single sign-on (SSO/SAML), Nimbus doesn't store your password — reset it through your identity provider (for example Okta or Google Workspace), then sign in with the SSO button.

chunks.title: Reset Your Password
chunks.text:
**Didn't get the email?** Check your spam folder, confirm you entered the address you signed up with, and make sure no typos crept in. Reset emails can take a few minutes. If it still hasn't arrived after 15 minutes, request another link or contact support — we can trigger a reset manually.

For security, password reset links can only be used once and expire after 60 minutes.
